name: Housing Foundation Of America, Lauderhill Florida
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit offering free homebuyer counseling, credit education, and down payment assistance across Florida and North Carolina.
description_long:
Housing Foundation of America, Inc. is a HUD-approved 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that has been operating for 10 years, providing housing and financial counseling services throughout Florida and select North Carolina markets. The organization positions itself as a comprehensive resource for first-time homebuyers and individuals seeking to improve their financial standing.

HFoA offers a diverse range of free and educational services including HUD-certified homebuyer workshops, credit essentials courses, budgeting education, foreclosure prevention counseling, reverse mortgage guidance, and property listing resources. They provide pre-purchase and post-purchase housing counseling, debt management, mortgage delinquency resolution, and loss mitigation services. The organization also operates homebuyer clubs, offers down payment assistance program alerts, and maintains partnerships with lenders, realtors, and minority builders coalitions. Their 8-hour certificate course follows the Fannie Mae Framework, and they offer both in-person (now virtual) and online eHome America training.

What distinguishes HFoA is its multi-state operational footprint with service areas spanning Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, St. Lucie, Martin, Brevard, Lee, and Orange Counties in Florida, plus multiple North Carolina counties including Guilford, Mecklenburg, Forsyth, and Wake. They combine traditional counseling with digital tools, offering homebuyer essentials videos, home inspection resources, and foreclosure scam awareness education. Their reverse mortgage specialization is notable, with dedicated workshops and expertise in this often-confusing financial product.

As a legitimate non-profit, HFoA provides genuine free counseling aligned with consumer protection standards. However, their website infrastructure appears underdeveloped—the site notes it is "not activated" and content organization is repetitive. There is minimal concrete information about counselor credentials, response times, or program outcomes. The geographic coverage spans multiple states but with unclear service boundaries, and specific details about down payment assistance amounts or qualifications are not transparently displayed on the main website.
pros: HUD-certified organization ensuring services meet federal housing counseling standards
Completely free services—no fees for homebuyer counseling, workshops, or credit education
Multi-state operation covering 8+ Florida counties plus North Carolina markets
Fannie Mae Framework certification for their 8-hour homebuyer education course
Specialized reverse mortgage counseling with dedicated weekly workshop series
Comprehensive service menu including foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Multiple engagement formats: in-person workshops, virtual Zoom sessions, online courses, and podcasts
cons: Website states 'not activated' with poor navigation and repetitive content structure
Limited transparency on counselor qualifications, certifications, or staff expertise details
No visible information about program eligibility requirements or application processes
Down payment assistance program details obscured behind portal sign-up; amounts and qualifications unclear
Sparse details on average response times, wait periods, or service delivery timelines
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking free HUD-approved counseling across Florida and North Carolina
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency needing loss mitigation assistance
Borrowers with poor credit interested in free credit rebuilding education and budgeting coaching
Seniors researching reverse mortgages who want unbiased HUD-certified guidance
diagnosis: Housing Foundation of America is best for first-time homebuyers and financially struggling homeowners who need free, trustworthy, HUD-certified counseling without sales pressure. The main caveat is that their website lacks user-friendliness and transparency about program specifics, eligibility, and operational details—prospective clients should contact them directly for clear information about services in their area and application procedures.
services: Credit essentials courses and credit rebuilding education
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation services
Mortgage delinquency and default resolution counseling
Down payment assistance program alerts and client portal
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
